Another example: [1, 2, 5, 3, 4, 6]. Initial start is 2 (5>3), initial end is 4 (4 < 6). The max of the unsorted part (indices 2-4) is 5. Now, check elements after initial end (index 4 is 4, next element is 6 which is greater than 5. So no adjustment. So end remains 4. The subarray is from 2 to 4. When sorted, the array becomes [1,2,3,4,5,6], which is correct. So code works.
